pattern: Since the tuple distance simplifies to twice the gap between the outermost chosen indices, the best triple for any value is three of its consecutive positions.
hints: Expand abs(i-j)+abs(j-k)+abs(k-i) for sorted indices i<j<k and notice the middle index cancels out.
The distance is just 2*(k-i), so among indices that share a value you want the smallest span covering three of them.

Group indices by value; for each value with at least three occurrences scan consecutive triples of its sorted positions, take 2*(positions[t+2]-positions[t]), and keep the global minimum (or -1 if none).

edge_inputs.description: best triple is non-adjacent in the array
single element cannot form a tuple
four equal, choose the tightest three

stress.description: medium array over a small value range so many values repeat
stress.note: narrow range guarantees triples appear frequently
